claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 9 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 9 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions about your circumstances and any children you're responsible for.

Let me start with: **Do you have any children that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you please tell me:
- How many children?
- Their names and ages?

This will help me understand your situation better.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. My son Alex is 9 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have one child, Alex, who is 9 years old.

Now I need to ask you a few more questions to assess your eligibility:

**Does Alex normally live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't live with me. He lives with his mum.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since Alex lives with his mum rather than with you, I need to understand the situation a bit more.

**Does anyone else currently claim Child Benefit for Alex?** (This would typically be his mum or whoever he lives with)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, his mum claims Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. 

I understand that Alex lives with his mum and she is already claiming Child Benefit for him. This is an important factor in determining your eligibility.

Just to complete the assessment, I have one more question:

**Do you contribute to Alex's upkeep?** For example, do you pay maintenance or child support for him?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do contribute to his upkeep. I help out with things like clothes and school expenses - I give £30 a week.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on what you've told me:
- Alex is 9 years old (under 16, so within the age criteria)
- Alex lives with his mum, not with you
- His mum already claims Child Benefit for him
- You contribute £30 per week towards his upkeep

According to the Child Benefit rules, **only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child at any given time**. Since Alex's mum is already claiming Child Benefit for him, you cannot also claim it for Alex, even though you contribute to his upkeep.

If Alex's mum were to stop claiming Child Benefit, and if Alex came to live with you or you became his main carer, then you could potentially claim it. But in the current situation where he lives with his mum and she claims the benefit, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit or your situation?

reasonings: Alex is 9 years old, which meets the age requirement (under 16). However, Alex does not live with the claimant - he lives with his mother. His mother currently claims Child Benefit for Alex. Under UK Child Benefit rules, only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child at any given time. Although the claimant contributes £30 per week to Alex's upkeep, this does not make them eligible to claim Child Benefit when another person (the mother) is already claiming it and the child lives with that other person. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (9 years old), who lives with his mother. The mother currently claims Child Benefit for Alex. Since only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child at any time, and Alex's mother is already claiming it, the claimant is not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex, despite contributing to his upkeep.

Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
